scenario_results.reason: The lane advanced reservation and order work through readiness, service execution, payment confirmation, and closure only when guest, ticket, and payment evidence were explicit.
Missing reservation, order, stage, and ticket evidence kept the work blocked and produced an exception note rather than an unsupported service recommendation.
Completion was refused until settlement, remake closure, and guest-recovery artifacts existed, keeping lifecycle transitions reversible when evidence failed.
Conflicting booking, tender, and order-state records were treated as a blocked payment mismatch and did not result in unsupported closure or refund guidance.
Out-of-policy refund and fee-waiver authority was rejected cleanly and routed to the correct guest-services or approver path.
Allergen and suspicious payment signals moved the case into escalation posture and preserved both food-safety and loss-prevention boundaries.
High-volume multi-channel service work produced a throughput-aware execution summary without dropping order-level traceability or exception handling.
The lane preserved restaurant terminology and mapped it to explicit reservation, order, service, and closure artifacts instead of drifting into generic hospitality language.
Pricing and revenue authority remained outside the lane and was routed to the correct adjacent owner.
Low-confidence handling correctly requested tenant refund, deposit, service-recovery, and safety authority facts rather than guessing.
Conflicting public, franchise, and tenant policy signals were summarized and escalated instead of being merged into a false confident answer.
Tenant-specific PMS folio and room-charge adaptation preserved the public restaurant baseline and explicit assumptions.

notes: All 12 scenarios passed against the standard 0.90 threshold while keeping the lane specific to restaurant reservations, guest checks, service execution, refund and fee boundaries, food-safety stop conditions, and completion evidence.
Functionality coverage includes all shared abilities, all execution-processing-and-fulfillment abilities, and all retail-consumer-and-hospitality abilities required by the audit contract.
